message:
fix(io): simplify stream line reader by removing unnecessary split_off/restore

The Lines stream implementation was splitting the buffer at each newline
position using split_off(), processing the line, then restoring the
remainder back into self.buf. This was unnecessary complexity because
step 4 of the read loop already guarantees that when we find a newline,
it is always at the very end of the buffer — there is never a remainder
to split off and restore.

Changes:
- Remove split_off() call and rest variable in the newline-found branch
- Remove the "Restore remainder" assignment (self.buf = rest)
- Mark the position variable as unused (_pos) since we no longer index
  into the buffer with it — we simply pop the trailing newline byte
- Add clarifying comment explaining why pop() is correct: the newline
  is always exactly at the end because of the step-4 invariant

This eliminates an allocation (split_off creates a new Vec) on every
line yielded from the stream, which is a meaningful improvement for
high-throughput line-oriented I/O.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
